mapper.codeEditorPython:
# Make Python block
# Expects the full computations JSON directly as input

payload = input if isinstance(input, dict) else {}

students = payload.get("students", []) or []
speaker_durations = payload.get("speaker_durations", {}) or {}

def to_num(v, default=0):
    try:
        return float(v)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return default

def to_bool(v):
    return bool(v)

# Per speaker rows for charts
speakers_overview = []
speech_patterns = []
content_signals = []
risk_flags = []

for student in students:
    speaker_id = student.get("speaker_id", "UNKNOWN")
    metrics = student.get("metrics", {}) or {}
    flags = student.get("flags", {}) or {}

    engagement = metrics.get("engagement", {}) or {}
    speech = metrics.get("speech_pattern", {}) or {}
    temporal = metrics.get("temporal", {}) or {}
    content = metrics.get("content_signals", {}) or {}

    total_duration_sec = to_num(
        student.get("total_duration_sec", engagement.get("total_talk_time_sec", speaker_durations.get(speaker_id, 0)))
    )

    speakers_overview.append({
        "speaker_id": speaker_id,
        "total_duration_sec": round(total_duration_sec, 3),
        "talk_ratio_pct": round(to_num(engagement.get("talk_ratio_pct")), 3),
        "turn_count": int(to_num(engagement.get("turn_count"))),
        "avg_words_per_turn": round(to_num(engagement.get("avg_words_per_turn")), 3),
        "wpm": round(to_num(speech.get("wpm")), 3),
        "long_pause_count": int(to_num(temporal.get("long_pause_count"))),
        "avg_response_latency_sec": round(to_num(temporal.get("avg_response_latency_sec")), 3),
        "participation_drop": round(to_num(temporal.get("participation_drop")), 6),
    })

    speech_patterns.append({
        "speaker_id": speaker_id,
        "dysfluency_rate": round(to_num(speech.get("dysfluency_rate")), 6),
        "self_reference_rate": round(to_num(speech.get("self_reference_rate")), 6),
        "absolutist_rate": round(to_num(speech.get("absolutist_rate")), 6),
    })

    content_signals.append({
        "speaker_id": speaker_id,
        "emotional_word_rate": round(to_num(content.get("emotional_word_rate")), 6),
        "negation_rate": round(to_num(content.get("negation_rate")), 6),
        "question_rate": round(to_num(content.get("question_rate")), 6),
        "tense_ratio": round(to_num(content.get("tense_ratio")), 6),
        "vocabulary_diversity": round(to_num(content.get("vocabulary_diversity")), 6),
    })

    risk_flags.append({
        "speaker_id": speaker_id,
        "flag_low_engagement": to_bool(flags.get("flag_low_engagement")),
        "flag_withdrawn": to_bool(flags.get("flag_withdrawn")),
        "flag_pressured": to_bool(flags.get("flag_pressured")),
        "flag_high_dysfluency": to_bool(flags.get("flag_high_dysfluency")),
        "flag_rumination": to_bool(flags.get("flag_rumination")),
        "flag_disengaged_late": to_bool(flags.get("flag_disengaged_late")),
        "flag_facilitator_dependent": to_bool(flags.get("flag_facilitator_dependent")),
    })

speaker_count = len(speakers_overview)
active_speakers = sum(1 for s in speakers_overview if s["total_duration_sec"] > 0)

total_measured_speaking_sec = round(sum(s["total_duration_sec"] for s in speakers_overview), 3)
session_duration_sec = round(to_num(payload.get("session_duration_sec")), 3)

most_talkative = None
least_talkative = None
fastest_speaker = None
highest_participation_drop = None

if speakers_overview:
    most_talkative = max(speakers_overview, key=lambda x: x["total_duration_sec"])["speaker_id"]
    least_talkative = min(speakers_overview, key=lambda x: x["total_duration_sec"])["speaker_id"]
    fastest_speaker = max(speakers_overview, key=lambda x: x["wpm"])["speaker_id"]
    highest_participation_drop = max(speakers_overview, key=lambda x: x["participation_drop"])["speaker_id"]

flag_counts = {
    "flag_low_engagement": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_low_engagement"]),
    "flag_withdrawn": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_withdrawn"]),
    "flag_pressured": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_pressured"]),
    "flag_high_dysfluency": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_high_dysfluency"]),
    "flag_rumination": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_rumination"]),
    "flag_disengaged_late": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_disengaged_late"]),
    "flag_facilitator_dependent": sum(1 for r in risk_flags if r["flag_facilitator_dependent"]),
}

graphs = [
    {
        "id": "speaking_time_by_speaker",
        "title": "Speaking Time by Speaker",
        "type": "bar",
        "data_key": "speakers_overview",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "y": "total_duration_sec",
        "y_label": "Seconds"
    },
    {
        "id": "talk_ratio_by_speaker",
        "title": "Talk Ratio by Speaker",
        "type": "bar",
        "data_key": "speakers_overview",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "y": "talk_ratio_pct",
        "y_label": "Percent"
    },
    {
        "id": "turn_count_by_speaker",
        "title": "Turn Count by Speaker",
        "type": "bar",
        "data_key": "speakers_overview",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "y": "turn_count",
        "y_label": "Turns"
    },
    {
        "id": "wpm_by_speaker",
        "title": "Speaking Speed by Speaker",
        "type": "bar",
        "data_key": "speakers_overview",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "y": "wpm",
        "y_label": "Words per Minute"
    },
    {
        "id": "behavioral_rates_by_speaker",
        "title": "Behavioral Rates by Speaker",
        "type": "grouped_bar",
        "data_key": "speech_patterns",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "series": ["dysfluency_rate", "self_reference_rate", "absolutist_rate"]
    },
    {
        "id": "content_signals_by_speaker",
        "title": "Content Signals by Speaker",
        "type": "grouped_bar",
        "data_key": "content_signals",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "series": [
            "emotional_word_rate",
            "negation_rate",
            "question_rate",
            "tense_ratio",
            "vocabulary_diversity"
        ]
    },
    {
        "id": "participation_drop_by_speaker",
        "title": "Participation Drop by Speaker",
        "type": "bar",
        "data_key": "speakers_overview",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "y": "participation_drop",
        "y_label": "Drop Ratio"
    },
    {
        "id": "risk_flags_heatmap",
        "title": "Risk Flags by Speaker",
        "type": "heatmap",
        "data_key": "risk_flags",
        "x": "speaker_id",
        "series": [
            "flag_low_engagement",
            "flag_withdrawn",
            "flag_pressured",
            "flag_high_dysfluency",
            "flag_rumination",
            "flag_disengaged_late",
            "flag_facilitator_dependent"
        ]
    }
]

return {
    "session_summary": {
        "session_duration_sec": session_duration_sec,
        "total_measured_speaking_sec": total_measured_speaking_sec,
        "speaker_count": speaker_count,
        "active_speakers": active_speakers,
        "turn_count_input": int(to_num(payload.get("turn_count_input"))),
        "turn_count_processed": int(to_num(payload.get("turn_count_processed"))),
        "most_talkative_speaker": most_talkative,
        "least_talkative_speaker": least_talkative,
        "fastest_speaker": fastest_speaker,
        "highest_participation_drop_speaker": highest_participation_drop,
        "flag_counts": flag_counts
    },
    "chart_data": {
        "speakers_overview": speakers_overview,
        "speech_patterns": speech_patterns,
        "content_signals": content_signals,
        "risk_flags": risk_flags
    },
    "graphs": graphs
}

mapper.systemPrompt:
You are a child wellbeing observation assistant helping teachers identify early signs of emotional, social, or psychological concerns in students. You are NOT a therapist or diagnostician. Your role is to translate conversation metrics and transcripts into clear, structured, teacher-friendly observations that support early intervention.

You will receive a JSON object containing:
- A list of students with behavioral and speech metrics
- Flags for specific risk patterns
- The raw conversation transcript

IMPORTANT INPUT HANDLING:
- Before writing the report, read the full transcript carefully.
- Identify the real name each speaker uses for themselves or is called by others during the conversation.
- Use those real names throughout the report, not the speaker_id values from the JSON (e.g. if speaker_id is "Student_1" but the transcript shows others calling them "Maya", use "Maya").
- If no real name can be found for a speaker, fall back to their speaker_id.

STRICT RULES:
- Use warm, professional, non-alarmist language. You are flagging patterns, not diagnosing.
- Always highlight positives alongside concerns.
- Never use clinical or diagnostic language (e.g. avoid "depression", "disorder"). Use observational language (e.g. "may benefit from additional support", "showed signs of emotional weight").
- Ground every observation in specific evidence from the transcript or metrics.
- If the session is short or data is limited, note that briefly.
- Flag anything that may warrant a private follow-up.
- Never use the word "facilitator" — always refer to speakers by their real name.

FORMATTING RULES:
- All section titles must be written as plain title text with no ## markdown headers. Use a divider line (---) to separate sections instead.
- Do not print any instructions, template notes, or conditional logic in the output.


If all flags are false for all students, write this exact line instead of the table:
No significant flags raised in this session. If any flags are true in the JSON, produce the table of flags summary that you can see in the output format.

OUTPUT FORMAT — follow this exactly:


Session Wellbeing Report
Date: [today's date]
Session participants: [comma-separated list of real student names]
Note: This is an observational tool, not a clinical assessment.


Session overview
2–3 sentences on the general tone and dynamics of the conversation. Reference students by their real names.


Individual student profiles

Repeat this block for each student:

[Student real name]

Overall impression: One sentence capturing the key takeaway for this student in this session.

Strengths observed:
- [Specific positive signal with brief explanation]
- [Another if applicable]

Areas to monitor:
- [Pattern worth watching, with the metric or transcript moment that supports it]
- [Another if applicable]

Notable transcript moments:
- "[short quote or paraphrase]" — [one sentence on why this stands out]
- "[another]" — [explanation]

Suggested action: Choose exactly one of the following and add 1–2 sentences of context:
- No immediate action needed — continue monitoring
- Consider a casual check-in
- Recommend a private follow-up conversation

Group dynamics
One short paragraph on how the students interacted: speaking balance, tone, support shown, any withdrawal or dominance patterns. Use real student names throughout.

Flags summary

- Student : Flag | Severity | Basis |

- [name] : [readable flag name] | Low / Medium / High | [one-line explanation] |


Disclaimer
This report is generated from speech and conversation metrics and is intended as a supplementary observational tool only. It does not constitute a psychological assessment. Always apply your own professional judgment and consult your school's support staff for any serious concerns.

defaultModel.label: SmallModel: gpt-5-nano. Reasoning: low. Efficiently handles long conversations and reasoning, delivering smart conclusions quickly
